questions.text: The branch of biology which deals with the organisms living in freshwater bodies is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Environmental Biology is a Physical Science at the intersection of environmental science, ecology, evolution, and global change. Environmental biology examines the ways organisms, species, and communities influence, and are impacted by, natural and human-altered ecosystems.

Microbiology is the study of the biology of microscopic organisms - viruses, bacteria, algae, fungi, slime molds, and protozoa. The methods used to study and manipulate these minute and mostly unicellular organisms differ from those used in most other biological investigations.
Ecology is the study of organisms and how they interact with the environment around them. An ecologist studies the relationship between living things and their habitats.

Freshwater biology is the scientific biological study of freshwater ecosystems and is a branch of limnology. This field seeks to understand the relationships between living organisms in their physical environment. These physical environments may include rivers, lakes, streams, ponds, lakes, reservoirs,

questions.explanation: A. Study of organisms in relation to their environment with respect to human impact. B. Study of microorganisms, viruses, protozoa, etc. C. Ecology is the study of interactions between organisms in their environment.

questions.text: This is a branch of biology which deals with the parasites:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Human biology is the study of all aspects of the human organism including evolution, genetics, ecology, anatomy and physiology, development, anthropology, and nutrition, among others.

Sociobiology is the study of the biological basis of social behavior among animals and humans. In recent years, sociologists have begun incorporating key ideas and explanatory principles from sociobiology into sociology.

Parasitology is the study of the interaction between parasites and their hosts. In general, parasitologists tend to concentrate on eukaryotic parasites, such as lice, mites, protozoa and worms, with prokaryotic parasites and other infectious agents the focusof fields such as bacteriology, microbiology and virology.

Biotechnology is the use of biology to develop new products, methods and organisms intended to improve human health and society. Biotechnology, often referred to as biotech, has existed since the beginning of civilization with the domestication of plants, animals and the discovery of fermentation.

questions.explanation: A. Deals with the study of man B. Deals with the study of human behavior in human beings. D. Use of living organisms, systems, or processes in manufacturing and service industries.

questions.text: The part of earth inhabited by living organisms including land, ocean, and atmosphere:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: The biosphere is made up of the parts of Earth where life exists. The biosphere extends from the deepest root systems of trees, to the dark environment of ocean trenches, to lush rainforests and high mountaintops. Scientists describe Earth in terms of spheres.

An ecosystem is a geographic area where plants, animals, and other organisms, as well as weather and landscape, work together to form a bubble of life. Ecosystems contain biotic or living, parts, as well as abiotic factors, or nonliving parts. Biotic factors include plants, animals, and other organisms.
A social group of any size whose members reside in a specific locality, share government, and often have a common cultural and historical heritage. a locality inhabited by such a group.
A habitat is an environment where an organism lives throughout the year or for shorter periods of time to find a mate. The habitat contains all an animal needs to survive such as food and shelter.

questions.explanation: B. A community together with its non-living surroundings. C. Two or more populations of different species living and interacting in the same area. D. Actual place where an organism lives in an ecosystem.

questions.text: The members of one species inhabiting the same area are called?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Population, in biology, is defined as all the organisms of the same group or species that live in a specific area and are capable of breeding among themselves. The term “population” encompasses a group of organisms of a single species that can interbreed and live in the same time in the same environment.

Multicellular organisms are those composed by multiple cells. They are classified in 13 major groups of terrestrial living beings, including animals, plants, fungi, ciliates, algae, and foraminifera. The number of cells per organism range from some tens to up to several million.

An organ (from the Latin "organum" meaning an instrument or tool) is a collection of tissues that structurally form a functional unit specialized to perform a particular function. Your heart, kidneys, and lungs are examples of organs
A species is often defined as a group of individuals that actually or potentially interbreed in nature. In this sense, a species is the biggest gene pool possible under natural conditions.

questions.explanation: A. Population consists of members of one species inhabiting the same area. B. Multicellular Organism is an individual living thing composed of many cells. C. An organ is a structure normally composed of several tissue types. D. Species are organisms that are very similar and carry the potential of inbreeding.

questions.text: Which of these is an example of the tissue level in plant:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Multicellular, complex animals have four primary types of tissue: epithelial, connective, muscle, and nervous.
Multicellular, complex animals have four primary types of tissue: epithelial, connective, muscle, and nervous.
Liver is an organ

Dermal tissue, for example, is a simple tissue that covers the outer surface of the plant and controls gas exchange. Vascular tissue is an example of a complex tissue, and is made of two specialized conducting tissues: xylem and phloem.

questions.explanation: A. Animal tissue B Animal tissue C. An organ in animals

questions.text: Which of these is an example of organ level in plants:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: At the most, we might distinguish roots, stems, leaves, and reproductive structures.
Stomach is an organ in an animal.
At the most, we might distinguish roots, stems, leaves and reproductive structures.
At the most, we might distinguish roots, stems, leaves and reproductive structures.

questions.explanation: Xylem is a vascular tissue in plants that conveys water and dissolved minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant and also provides physical support. B. Organ in animals Mesenchyme is a type of loosely organized animal embryonic connective tissue of undifferentiated cells that gives rise to blood and lymph vessels, bone, and muscle.

questions.text: A city named Lahore is an example of which level of organization?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: A social group of any size whose members reside in a specific locality, share government, and often have a common cultural and historical heritage. a locality inhabited by such a group.

Population is defined as all nationals present in, or temporarily absent from a country, and aliens permanently settled in a country. This indicator shows the number of people that usually live in an area. Growth rates are the annual changes in population resulting from births, deaths and net migration during the year.

An ecosystem is a geographic area where plants, animals, and other organisms, as well as weather and landscape, work together to form a bubble of life. Ecosystems contain biotic or living, parts, as well as abiotic factors, or nonliving parts. Biotic factors include plants, animals, and other organisms.
A species is often defined as a group of individuals that actually or potentially interbreed in nature. In this sense, a species is the biggest gene pool possible under natural conditions.

questions.explanation: A. Community refers to the living part of an ecosystem B. Population is a group of living organisms of the same species. C. Ecosystem is a community with its non-living surroundings. D. Species are organisms that are very similar and carry the potential of inbreeding.

questions.text: A large regional community primarily determined by climate is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Mutualism refers to a type of symbiotic relationship between two different species in which both benefit from the interaction. It does not describe a large regional community primarily determined by climate.

Competition: Competition refers to the interaction between organisms where they compete for limited resources such as food, water, or territory. While competition can occur within a biome, it does not define a large regional community solely based on climate.

The correct answer is a biome. A large regional community primarily determined by climate is referred to as a biome. A biome is a specific geographic area characterized by distinct climatic conditions, such as temperature, precipitation, and seasonal variations. The plant and animal species found within a biome are adapted to the prevailing environmental conditions of that region.

Parasitism: Parasitism is a type of symbiotic relationship in which one organism (parasite) benefits at the expense of another organism (host). Parasitism is not specific to a particular climate or region but can occur in various ecosystems.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is a biome. A large regional community primarily determined by climate is referred to as a biome. A biome is a specific geographic area characterized by distinct climatic conditions, such as temperature, precipitation, and seasonal variations. The plant and animal species found within a biome are adapted to the prevailing environmental conditions of that region.

questions.text: The number and variety of species in a place is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: A biome is an area classified according to the species that live in that location. Temperature range, soil type, and the amount of light and water are unique to a particular place and form the niches for specific species allowing scientists to define the biome. However, scientists disagree on how many biomes exist.

The biosphere is made up of the parts of Earth where life exists. The biosphere extends from the deepest root systems of trees to the dark environment of ocean trenches, to lush rainforests and high mountaintops. Scientists describe Earth in terms of spheres.

Biodiversity is all the different kinds of life you'll find in one area—the variety of animals, plants, fungi, and even microorganisms like bacteria that make up our natural world. Each of these species and organisms work together in ecosystems, like an intricate web, to maintain balance and support life.
A phyletic lineage is an unbroken series of species arranged in ancestor to descendant sequence, with each later species having evolved from the one which immediately preceded it.

questions.explanation: A. A large regional community primarily determined by climate. B. Part of earth inhabited by living organisms D. Lineage which shows phylectic linkages

questions.text: If we accept that birds have wings and the sparrows are birds then we conclude sparrows have wings is a example of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Inductive reasoning is a logical process in which multiple premises, all believed true or found true most of the time, are combined to obtain a specific conclusion. Inductive reasoning is often used in applications that involve prediction, forecasting, or behavior.

Deductive reasoning is a logical approach where you progress from general ideas to specific conclusions. It's often contrasted with inductive reasoning, where you start with specific observations and form general conclusions. Deductive reasoning is also called deductive logic.
An assumption or concession made for the sake of argument.
Science is the pursuit and application of knowledge and understanding of the natural and social world following a systematic methodology based on evidence.

questions.explanation: A. Reasoning from specific to general C. A statement per experience and background knowledge D. A systemized knowledge

questions.text: Hardy Weinberg is an example of which entity:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: There is soon a series of hypotheses supported by the results of many tests which are then called a theory
A biological rule or biological law is a generalized law, principle, or rule of thumb formulated to describe patterns observed in living organisms.
An assumption or concession made for the sake of argument.
An act or instance of noticing or perceiving.

questions.explanation: A. Series of hypotheses supported by tests C. Statement per experience and background knowledge D. Based on experimental inquiry

questions.text: Production of genetically identical copies of organisms by asexual reproduction is called as:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Cloning is a technique scientists use to make exact genetic copies of living things. Genes, cells, tissues, and even whole animals can all be cloned. Some clones already exist in nature. Single-celled organisms like bacteria make exact copies of themselves each time they reproduce.

Genetic engineering (also called genetic modification) is a process that uses laboratory-based technologies to alter the DNA makeup of an organism. This may involve changing a single base pair (A-T or C-G), deleting a region of DNA or adding a new segment of DNA.
Biological control is the use by humans of beneficial insects such as predators and parasitoids, or pathogens such as fungi and viruses, to control unwanted insects, weeds, or diseases.

Tissue culture (TC) is the cultivation of plant cells, tissues, or organs on specially formulated nutrient media. Under the right conditions, an entire plant can be regenerated from a single cell. Plant tissue culture is a technique that has been around for more than 30 years.

questions.explanation: B. Genes with desirable characters are produced C Control by some living organisms D. Culturing tissues using specific medium

questions.text: Which one of the following is an example of a contagious disease transmitted by blood contact?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Whooping cough, also known as pertussis, is a highly contagious respiratory infection caused by the bacterium Bordetella pertussis. It is primarily transmitted through respiratory droplets when an infected person coughs or sneezes. It is not primarily transmitted through blood contact.

Polio, also known as poliomyelitis, is a viral infection caused by the poliovirus. It is primarily transmitted through the fecal-oral route, meaning it spreads through contaminated food, water, or contact with an infected person's feces. It is not primarily transmitted through blood contact.

Anemia refers to a condition characterized by a decrease in the number of red blood cells or a decrease in their ability to carry oxygen. Anemia can have various causes, such as nutritional deficiencies, chronic diseases, or genetic disorders. It is not an infectious disease and is not transmitted through blood contact.

Hepatitis is an example of a contagious disease that can be transmitted through blood contact. Hepatitis refers to the inflammation of the liver and can be caused by various viruses, including hepatitis B and hepatitis C. These viruses can be spread through direct contact with infected blood or bodily fluids, such as sharing needles or syringes, receiving contaminated blood transfusions, or engaging in unprotected sexual activity with an infected person.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is d) hepatitis. Hepatitis is an example of a contagious disease that can be transmitted through blood contact, primarily through direct contact with infected blood or bodily fluids. Whooping cough, polio, and anemia are not primarily transmitted through blood contact.
